sourceSummary: Portfolio site, CV/LinkedIn information provided by Dinupa Devinda, public GitHub repositories, and public LinkedIn project links.

profile.name: Dinupa Devinda
profile.headline: Machine Learning | AI/ML Developer | R&D Engineer
profile.summary: Machine learning focused background in engineering, computer science, and mathematics. Experienced in building ML workflows from data preparation and model development to evaluation and deployment-focused implementation. Interested in intelligent, data-driven solutions for engineering and technology problems.

strengths: Machine learning workflow development, model evaluation, and deployment experiments
Computer vision, audio classification, IMU/sensor data, and applied ML projects
Engineering systems involving embedded devices, sensors, telemetry, dashboards, and APIs
Python, TensorFlow/Keras, PyTorch, scikit-learn, XGBoost, OpenCV, TFLite, ONNX, React, Spring Boot, Go, C/C++, Java, and SQL
Basic LLM application integration through a portfolio chatbot using Gemini, Cloudflare Workers, CORS, secret handling, and context-grounded prompting

education.degree: BSc Eng (Hons), Electrical and Electronics Engineering
education.notes: Engineering background with work across automation, embedded systems, electronics, machine learning, computer vision, and sensor-based projects.

education.subjectCombination: Computer Science, Pure Mathematics, and Applied Mathematics
education.notes: Computer science and double mathematics background supporting programming, algorithms, modelling, and machine learning foundations.

education.status: Certificate in Business Accounting completed; reading for Dip MA (Operational Level)
education.notes: Business, economics, management accounting, and finance foundation.

education.activities: Chess player; U17, U15, and U13 school chess team member.

experience.summary: Developed C/C++ firmware for ESP32 edge devices with A7680C 4G modem AT-command handling, MQTT/MQTTS, HTTP, SMTP, SMS, and call support. Built telemetry pipelines using MQTT/HTTP and JSON payloads. Worked with ThingsBoard dashboards, web portals, MQTT Explorer, HiveMQ, Grafana exposure, and basic GitHub Actions/CI practice.

experience.location: Gampaha District, Western Province, Sri Lanka
experience.summary: Built C/C++ automation and embedded-control projects in an electronics manufacturing environment, including an Automated Guided Vehicle, flame-treatment automation upgrade, and automated PCB conveyor system. Gained exposure to SMT/THT manufacturing, ICT and functional testing, SPEA 3030/4050 test systems, FactoryLogix workflows, production data flow, and NPI processes.

projects.title: Vehicular Black Box: Vehicle Violation Detector
projects.type: Final-year integrated ML and embedded system
projects.summary: Raspberry Pi based vehicle black-box prototype combining camera, audio, IMU, GPS/GSM inputs, violation and driving-event modules, evidence upload, backend services, and a React dashboard.
projects.limitations: Prototype with validation notes and limitations documented. Do not describe it as a production-ready safety system.

projects.title: Road Sign Detection with YOLO and ONNX
projects.summary: Two-stage road-sign recognition pipeline. YOLO localizes candidate signs and an ONNX classifier separates speed limits, traffic lights, no-honking signs, and other signs for Raspberry Pi style deployment.

projects.title: Vehicle Horn Detection with Audio CNN
projects.summary: Vehicle horn detector using 1-second microphone windows, log-mel features, CNN training, and TensorFlow Lite export for lightweight inference.
projects.limitations: Internal metrics are strong; field validation is the next step.

projects.title: Crash Detection with Audio and IMU Sensor Fusion
projects.summary: Crash detection module combining audio and IMU signals. It packages separate model outputs into fusion labels for the vehicular black-box prototype.

projects.title: Lane Change Detection from IMU Data
projects.summary: Recall-focused lane-change detector using BMI160 accelerometer and gyroscope windows, motion features, and XGBoost on a public BYD driving-events dataset.

projects.title: Aggressive Driving Detection from IMU Signals
projects.summary: Sensor-based model for classifying normal versus aggressive driving behavior using yaw-rate and acceleration windows, model comparison, and Optuna-tuned XGBoost.

projects.title: A7680C 4G Modem Firmware for ESP32
projects.summary: C/C++ firmware for ESP32 edge devices using the A7680C LTE modem, with AT-command handling, MQTT/MQTTS, HTTP, SMTP, SMS, and call support.

projects.title: BRMS Boarding Room Management API
projects.summary: Java Spring Boot REST API for boarding-room management workflows with Spring Data JPA, SQL Server configuration, environment-based settings, and H2-backed testing.

projects.title: ServiceLink: Local Hiring Platform
projects.summary: Role-based service marketplace with Spring Boot APIs, React pages, JWT authentication, service listings, bookings, categories, and messaging.

projects.title: Smart Fish Habitat Management System
projects.type: Raspberry Pi automation and vision project
projects.summary: Automated aquarium system for water-quality monitoring, scheduled feeding, water changing, filtration, aeration, and heating. The project also tested YOLOv11 fish detection and TensorFlow Lite vision work for water-quality assessment.

portfolioAssistant.name: Portfolio chatbot
portfolioAssistant.shortAnswer: This chatbot is a simple RAG-style portfolio assistant. It is better described as single-file context RAG or context-injection RAG, not vector database RAG.
architecture: The portfolio remains a static Next.js export suitable for GitHub Pages.
A floating React chat widget sends visitor questions to a Cloudflare Worker /chat endpoint.
The Cloudflare Worker loads the public knowledge file public/portfolio-chat.json from the portfolio site.
The Worker sends the portfolio context and visitor question to Gemini.
The Gemini API key is stored only as a Cloudflare Worker secret, not in browser code.
The Worker validates message length, handles CORS, and returns concise answers with source labels.
whatItDemonstrates: Static frontend plus serverless backend architecture
Secure API key handling through Worker secrets
Prompt grounding using a compact portfolio knowledge file
Basic RAG-style thinking without embeddings or a vector database
portfolioAssistant.honestyNote: It should not be described as an advanced enterprise RAG system, a vector database system, or proof of production LLM platform experience. It is a practical portfolio assistant showing basic RAG-style implementation and LLM integration.

answeringRules: Answer only from this portfolio context.
If a fact is not included here, say that the portfolio does not include that information.
Questions about this chatbot, RAG, Gemini, Cloudflare Workers, or how the assistant works should be answered from the portfolioAssistant section.
Use only the listed education periods; do not add graduation-status claims that are not in this context.
Do not claim production deployment, safety certification, employment, awards, or metrics that are not listed.
Describe the chatbot as simple RAG-style/context-injection RAG, not advanced vector database RAG.
For recruiter questions, be concise and point to GitHub, LinkedIn, CV, or project pages when useful.
